zh.Brenchley, Kent, England: 英國英格蘭肯特布藍利
zh.lifeSummary: Sir William I Downer於1415年出生在英國英格蘭肯特布藍利。他和Horsmonden至少有1個兒子。他去世於1503年在他的家鄉享年88歲。

zh-hans.Brenchley, Kent, England: 英国英格兰肯特布藍利
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir William I Downer于1415年出生在英国英格兰肯特布藍利。他和Horsmonden至少有1个儿子。他去世于1503年在他的家乡享年88岁。

nameMeanings.name: Downer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Isle of Wight, Hampshire, and Sussex): topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or down, from Middle English douner, a derivative of Middle English doun (Old English dūn) ‘hill’ + -er. It is synonymous with atte Doune (see Down ) and is a type of formation especially common in Sussex, Surrey, and Hampshire.

English: in the north-West Midlands perhaps a variant of Downward with shortening of the final syllable to -er, a habitational name probably from Downwood (Herefordshire) and perhaps also Dunwood (Staffordshire), with -wood re-etymologized as -ward and occasionally shortened to -ard. See also Downard .

variants.name: Dunman
meanings: English: variant of Downman, a topographic name for someone who lived ‘(by the) down’; compare Downer . Alternatively, in some instances the name may be a late variant of Downham .

variants.name: Down
meanings: English (Devon): topographic name for someone who lived on or near a down or low hill, from Middle English dun(e), doun(e) (Old English dūn) ‘down, low hill’. See also Downer .

English (Devon): possibly in some cases a patronymic from the Old English personal name Dūn, but later names of this origin cannot reliably be distinguished from those originating from the topographic sense. See Dunn 2.

variants.name: Downard
meanings: English: variant of Downer , with excrescent -d. Downard was re-etymologized as Downeherd and Downward, and perhaps also as Dunwood.

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: George
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: Sir William I Downer was born in 1415, in Brenchley, Kent, England, United Kingdom. He had at least 1 son with Horsmonden. He died in 1503, in his hometown, at the age of 88.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir William I Downer est né en 1415, à Brenchley, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Horsmonden. Il est décédé en 1503, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 88 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Sir William I Downer wurde 1415 in Brenchley, Kent, England, United Kingdom geboren. Er hatte mit Horsmonden mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1503 im Alter von 88 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Sir William I Downer è nato il nel 1415 nel Brenchley, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Horsmonden. Morì nel 1503, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 88 anni.

ja.Brenchley, Kent, England: イギリスイギリスケントブレンチリー
ja.lifeSummary: Sir William I Downerは1415年にイギリスイギリスケントブレンチリーで生まれた。彼はHorsmondenとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1503年に故郷で88歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William I Downer(은)는 1415년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 브렌클리에서 태어났다. 그는 Horsmonden(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1503년 그의 고향에서 88세의 나이로 사망했다.

mn.Brenchley, Kent, England: Brenchley, Centlond, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Sir William I Downer nasceu em 1415, em Brenchley, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Horsmonden. Ele faleceu em 1503, em sua cidade natal, com 88 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir William I Downer родился в 1415 г. в местности бренчли, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Horsmonden. Он умер в 1503 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 88 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Sir William I Downer nació en 1415, en Brenchley, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Horsmonden. Murió en 1503, en su pueblo, a la edad de 88 años.
